name: SOL Asymmetric Trend Persistence v1
thesis: Transitioning SOL 4h markets can reward continuation if trigger, risk, and exit roles are separated instead of overloaded onto one indicator. The edge should come from persistent trend behavior with asymmetric exit logic and fast de-risk when follow-through stalls.
edge_mechanism: trend persistence with asymmetric exit structure
invalidation_condition: Abort if this remains too sparse or cannot produce durable post-cost edge in blind windows.

long: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.5 * ATR_14
short: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.5 * ATR_14
